title: CODEX Preparation of Reporter 96-Well Plates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2m7qb","text":"A CODEX® Reporter is a fluorescent dye conjugated to an oligonucleotide that is complementary to a specific oligonucleotide barcoded antibody. This protocol describes how to prepare 96-well plates of Reporter Mix for use in a CODEX® run. Each well contains Reporters are grouped together in mixtures of three per cycle plus the nuclear stain. During each cycle the Codex instrument dispenses one well on the tissue sample and images the 3 markers and the nuclear stain. At the end of the cycle the Reporters are removed from the tissue by a gentle wash.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1drfg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2gg6n","text":"Plan the  CODEX experiment carefully to ensure that each CODEX/barcoded antibody has a unique barcode not shared with any other antibody in the entire  multicycle experiment.  Assign each antibody a cycle number ensuring that there is only one reporter labled with a given fluorescence/dye in each cycle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cgiuo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fudgb","text":"Each cycle can have one, two or three reporters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6tcpi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"asot0","text":"Blank cycles, (no reporter) containing nuclear stain only, should be included at the beginning and end of the experiment.  (i.e. cycle 1 and last cycle)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"n3q1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"acc6q","text":"Use the attached cycle template to plan the experiment and enter information into the COEX run software.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"thl0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8g3t3","text":"Use only the CODEX supplied 96-well Plates and Foil seals.  Other seals may stick to the instrument during sample loading steps.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eq7pp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8541o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ehbqo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cq3tq","text":"Thaw Assay reagent, Reporters, and Nulcear Stain and place on ice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1oh5b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"265t1","text":"Label a 1.5ml amber microcentrifuge tube for each cycle of the CODEX experiment. Include a Blank cycle at the beginning and end of the experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"eb6ve","text":"CODEX® KIT","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ae6c","text":"96-well Plates Akoya CODEX 232527","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fessb","text":"96-well Plate Foil Seals Akoya CODEX 232528","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpkn","text":"10X CODEX Buffer Akoya CODEX 232119","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"41vv","text":"Assay Reagent Akoya CODEX232120","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4itto","text":"Nuclear Stain AkoyaCODEX 232121","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"esfn9","text":"Reporters (appropriate for barcodes antibodies)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7vd8l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c3bo2","text":"Other Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a57c2","text":"Nuclease-Free Water (not DEPC Treated) Ambion AM9938","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8jajn","text":"Amber colored 1.5 ml tubes USA Scientific Sealrite 1615-5507","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bo7tn","text":"Ice bucket","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"54kju","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the Reporter Stock Solution (Based on the number of cycles and coverslips to be run ): %s %s 5
Prepare the Reporter Master Mix Aliquots for Each Cycle
Label an amber microcentrifuge tube for each cycle. Include tubes for the blank cycles at the beginning and end of the experiment.

Aliquot Reporter Stock Solution into each tube following the chart below (volumes will vary depending on the number of reporters used in an individual cycle (see CODEX Cycle Template). %s %s Reporter Solutions: Label a 1.5 ml amber tube for each cyc %s %s le %s %s
Add appropriate Reporters to the Reporter Stock Solution aliquots to make a Master Mix for each cycle
Briefly , Spin tubes using a bench top centrifuge. Return Reporters to ice.

Pipette Akoya Reporters into the labeled amber tubes containing Reporter Stock Solution. Use volumes listed in step 2.2. column A based on number of reporters in a particular cycle. Follow the cycle template or the reporter tray template (below). %s %s CODEX Cycle %s %s Templ %s %s at %s %s e %s %s Reporter Tray Template Cycle 1 Cycle 2 Cycle 3 Cycle 4 Cycle 5 + Cycle * Cycle*+1 dye/fluor Blank Antibody (Reporter 1) Antibody (Reporter 4) label any EMPTY channels Blank Cy7 Antibody (Reporter 2) Antibody (Reporter 5) Antibody (Reporter 7) 550 Antibody (Reporter 3) Antibody (Reporter 6) ... %s %s etc Cy %s %s 5 %s %s %s %s
Prepare the 96-well Reporter Plate: Pipette 245 ul of Reporter containing Master Mix into the corresponding cycle well . Filled 96-well Reporter Plate with foil seal strips in place

Use a different 96-well plate for each Reporter plate prepared. Lightly cover entire rows of wells (i.e. B1-B12) with strips of foil seal. Keep the filled 96-well plates IN THE DARK to avoid photo bleaching. Store at 4˚C for up to two weeks. 4 °C
creator: Jerelyn Nick
